Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Understand User Input Database PostgreSQL Schema Three tables order_event_log orders resources Assumed Normal Process Flow 1. Register Order 2. Perform Credit Check 3. Validate Stock 4. Confirm Shipment 5. Ship Goods 6. Issue Invoice 7. Receive Payment Example Data Four cases 1001 1002 1003 1004 with event logs showing various deviations Task Identify anomalies hypothesize causes propose SQL queries

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Data for Anomalies Mental Walkthrough Case 1001 Follows the exact normal flow Case 1002 Flow Register Order Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Issue Invoice Receive Payment Anomalies Credit Check and Validate Stock happen after Shipment Confirm Shipment happens before Credit Check and Stock Validation Case 1003 Flow Register Order Perform Credit Check Ship Goods Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Receive Payment Anomalies Missing Validate Stock Ship Goods happens before Confirm Shipment Case 1004 Flow Register Order Receive Payment Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Anomalies Payment received immediately after registration before invoice credit check stock validation or shipment

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Identify Anomalies Undesirable Behaviors Out-of-Sequence Execution Activities occurring in wrong order Missing Mandatory Steps Cases skipping critical controls like Perform Credit Check or Validate Stock Premature Late Activities Early shipment confirmation late shipment confirmation payment before invoicing Data State Inconsistencies shipment_scheduled N but Ship Goods still occurs Potential Fraud Policy Violations Bypassing credit checks for high-value orders processing payments prematurely

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 4. Hypothesize Causes System Integration Errors Event logging system might be capturing timestamps incorrectly or workflow engine isnt enforcing sequence constraints Policy Violations Workarounds Staff might be manually overriding steps to expedite orders especially priority orders like 1002 or bypassing credit checks for trusted customers Training Process Awareness Employees might not understand the required sequence leading to ad-hoc execution Data Entry Logging Issues additional_info flags like attempted_early Y or late_confirmation Y suggest manual overrides or system warnings that were ignored Fraud Financial Risk Receiving payment before invoicing or shipping could indicate accounting irregularities or system glitches

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 5. Develop SQL Queries for Investigation Need to write PostgreSQL queries Detect out-of-sequence activities Find missing mandatory steps Identify cases with specific flags Correlate anomalies with order value type or resource roles Check for timestamp inversions Query 1 Detect Out-of-Sequence Activities compare timestamps of specific activities per case Query 2 Identify Missing Mandatory Steps Query 3 Correlate Anomalies with Order Value Type Resources Query 4 Detect State Inconsistencies in additional_info Query 5 Analyze Resource Department Involvement in Violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self-Correction Refinement during drafting Check Query 1 conditional aggregation approach is good but handle NULLs properly refine it Hypotheses should map directly to queries Structure logically Self-Correction Verification during thought Check case 1004 Receive Payment happens at 09:05 right after Register Order highly anomalous Check case 1002 order_type is priority supports hypothesis that priority orders might be fast-tracked Check case 1003 Missing Validate Stock All steps look solid

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 6. Structure the Response Organize the output into Identified Anomalies Undesirable Behaviors Hypothesized Root Causes Proposed SQL Queries for Investigation with explanations of what each query does Recommendations Next Steps Ensure the SQL is PostgreSQL-compatible uses CTEs window functions appropriately All good Output matches response Proceed
